description: After completing FEAT-001 through FEAT-010, implement the next 10 highest-impact features based on competitive analysis. Focus: audio overviews, browser extension, reference manager export, team collaboration, public API, author network, trend analysis, gap detection, preprint tracking, and impact scoring.

userStories.title: Add audio overviews (AI podcast generation) for papers
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want to generate conversational audio summaries of papers or collections so I can consume research while commuting, like NotebookLM Audio Overviews.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /projects/{id}/audio-overviews generates dialogue script from paper content
Audio player with play/pause, seek, speed 0.5x-2x controls in UI
Generated audio cached and available for download
User can customize tone (formal/conversational) and focus areas
Tests: verify audio overview endpoint returns structured dialogue
Tests: verify audio player component renders with controls
userStories.notes: Backend: audio_overview_service.py with TTS generation. Frontend: AudioOverviewDialog + audio player component. Use OpenAI TTS or ElevenLabs API.

userStories.title: Add browser extension for one-click paper capture
userStories.description: As a researcher browsing academic sites, I want a Chrome/Firefox extension to save papers directly into my Omelette projects without leaving the page.
acceptanceCriteria: Extension detects DOI/arXiv ID/PDF URL and shows save button
One-click save fetches metadata via Semantic Scholar API
User can select target project and add tags from extension popup
Backend: POST /api/v1/upload/browser accepts PDF + metadata
Extension shows sync status with notifications
Tests: verify browser upload endpoint accepts PDF
userStories.notes: Build Chrome extension (Manifest V3). Content script for DOI detection. Popup for project selection. Backend: /api/v1/upload/browser endpoint. Reuse existing paper processing pipeline.

userStories.title: Add reference manager export (Zotero/Mendeley/BibTeX)
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want to export my Omelette collections to Zotero, Mendeley, or BibTeX so I can use them in my existing citation workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /projects/{id}/export/bibtex returns BibTeX formatted string
POST /projects/{id}/export/ris returns RIS formatted string
POST /projects/{id}/export/zotero creates Zotero collection via API
Export dialog shows preview of items before exporting
Tests: verify BibTeX export includes all required fields
Tests: verify RIS export is valid format
userStories.notes: Backend: export_service.py with BibTeX, RIS, Zotero API integration. Frontend: ExportDialog component. Reuse existing collection model.

userStories.title: Add team collaboration and shared workspaces
userStories.description: As a research team lead, I want shared project workspaces where team members can collaboratively annotate papers and track review assignments.
acceptanceCriteria: New team_members table with project_id, user_id, role columns
POST/GET/PUT/DELETE /projects/{id}/members for CRUD
Shared annotations visible to all project members with author attribution
Paper review assignment with due dates and status tracking
RBAC middleware restricts access by role
Tests: verify member CRUD operations
Tests: verify RBAC blocks unauthorized access
userStories.notes: New model: team_member.py. New service: team_service.py. RBAC middleware for existing endpoints. Frontend: InviteModal, MemberList, role selector.

userStories.title: Add public REST API with API key management
userStories.description: As a power user, I want a documented REST API with API keys so I can programmatically access Omelette features and integrate with my research pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: New api_keys table with hashed keys and scopes
POST/GET/DELETE /api/v1/api-keys for key management
API key auth middleware alongside session auth
Rate limiting enforced per API key
OpenAPI/Swagger docs served at /api/docs
Frontend: API Keys settings tab with generate/revoke UI
Tests: verify API key auth works
Tests: verify rate limiting enforced
userStories.notes: Backend: api_key model, auth middleware, rate limiting via slowapi. FastAPI auto-generates OpenAPI docs. Frontend: API Keys tab in SettingsPage.

userStories.title: Add author network and collaboration graph
userStories.description: As a researcher exploring a new field, I want to visualize author collaboration networks and identify influential researchers and research groups.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /projects/{id}/analysis/author-network returns author graph
Force-directed graph with authors as nodes, co-authorship as edges
Node size reflects publication count, edge weight reflects co-authorship count
Author detail panel shows papers, h-index estimate, top topics
Graph exportable as PNG/SVG
Tests: verify author network returns structured graph data
Tests: verify AuthorNetwork component renders correctly
userStories.notes: Backend: author_network_service.py extracts authors, builds co-authorship matrix, computes centrality metrics. Frontend: AuthorNetworkDialog reusing d3-force from citation-graph.

userStories.title: Add research trend analysis and temporal insights
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want to see how research topics have evolved over time with trend detection for emerging areas and declining topics.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /projects/{id}/analysis/trends returns year-binned topic data
Timeline chart showing publication volume per year
Topic trend lines with rising/declining indicators
Emerging topic detection (>50% YoY growth)
Export trend analysis as PNG or CSV
Frontend: TrendsPage with line/bar/area charts
Tests: verify trend endpoint returns structured data
Tests: verify trend chart renders correctly
userStories.notes: Backend: trend_service.py extracts keywords, computes year-binned aggregates, linear regression for trend slopes. Frontend: TrendsPage using recharts (already installed).

userStories.title: Add literature gap detection and research opportunity finder
userStories.description: As a researcher identifying my next project, I want Omelette to analyze my collection and identify under-explored areas and generate research questions.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /projects/{id}/analysis/gaps returns gap analysis results
Identifies 3-5 under-researched subtopics with supporting evidence
Generates 5-10 candidate research questions with novelty scores
User can save gaps as research ideas with notes
Analysis runs asynchronously with progress notification
Frontend: GapAnalysisPanel with gap cards
Tests: verify gap detection returns structured data
userStories.notes: Backend: gap_service.py uses LLM to analyze paper collection. Async task with SSE progress. Frontend: GapAnalysisPanel component. Reuse existing contradiction data as input.

userStories.title: Add preprint-to-journal version tracking
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want to automatically track when preprints in my collection are updated or published in journals, so I always cite the definitive version.
acceptanceCriteria: Daily job polls Semantic Scholar/CrossRef for paper updates
GET /papers/{id}/versions returns version history
Version timeline shows preprint v1, v2, journal publication
User can upgrade preprint to published version preserving annotations
Notification badge on papers with new versions available
Frontend: VersionTimeline component in paper detail
Tests: verify version tracking endpoint returns history
Tests: verify annotation migration on version upgrade
userStories.notes: Backend: version_service.py with daily APScheduler job. Frontend: VersionTimeline component. Use react-diff-viewer-continued for abstract diff display.

userStories.title: Add citation impact scoring and paper significance ranking
userStories.description: As a researcher evaluating papers, I want computed impact scores combining citation count, recency, journal prestige, and evidence consensus to prioritize significant papers.
acceptanceCriteria: Each paper displays Omelette Impact Score (0-100)
Score combines: citations (30%), recency (20%), journal IF (20%), evidence consensus (15%), field percentile (15%)
Papers sortable by impact score in all views
Score methodology transparent with factor breakdown tooltip
Impact score column in papers table
Tests: verify impact score calculation bounded [0,100]
Tests: verify score breakdown returns all factors
userStories.notes: Backend: compute_impact_score() in analysis service. Fetch citation counts from Semantic Scholar. Frontend: ImpactScoreBadge on paper cards, score column in tables.
